name: nmap
purpose: Network mapper — port scanning, service/version detection, OS fingerprinting, and scriptable network security auditing
author: Gordon Lyon (Fyodor)
why_notable: Gordon Lyon published nmap in Phrack #51 (September 1997) — the definitive underground hacker magazine — introducing techniques like SYN scanning, FIN/NULL/Xmas scans, decoy scanning, and fragmented packet scanning that were either new or previously undocumented publicly. Before nmap, scanning meant typing telnet commands one port at a time. Version 2.x (late 1990s) added OS fingerprinting by observing how TCP/IP stacks deviate from RFC 793 in unique ways; -sV (2001) added service/version detection turning nmap from a port scanner into a network intelligence tool; NSE (2006) added a Lua scripting engine with ~600 scripts ranging from banner grabs to CVE exploitation. Maintained continuously for 25+ years, it ships by default in Kali Linux, Parrot OS, and BlackArch. Its appearance in The Matrix Reloaded (2003) with a real nmap 2.54BETA7 command is one of the most technically accurate hacking sequences in mainstream film.
